topic: SU(3) flavor analysis of charmed-meson semileptonic decays and amplitude decomposition
subdomain: Flavor physics; SU(3) group theory; weak semileptonic decays
related_knowledge: SU(3) flavor representations: 3, 3bar, 8; tensor index notation
CKM matrix elements V_cd and V_cs in c → d,s semileptonic transitions
Spectator model for heavy-meson semileptonic decays
Pseudoscalar and vector light-meson octets (π,K,η8; ρ,K*,ω8/φ8)
task_background: There are six quark flavors. The light flavors u, d, s are approximately symmetric under SU(3) flavor (SU(3)_F) and can be organized as a triplet. Heavy quarks c, b are treated as SU(3)_F singlets. In semileptonic charm decays, the charm quark undergoes a weak charged-current transition c → q ℓ^+ ν_ℓ with q ∈ {d, s}, governed by CKM matrix elements V_cd and V_cs. Charmed mesons D form an SU(3)_F anti-triplet (3bar) with components D_1 = D^0(c ū), D_2 = D^+(c d̄), D_3 = D_s^+(c s̄). Light mesons made of a light quark and a light antiquark form an SU(3)_F octet (8), which applies both to pseudoscalar (P) and vector (V) nonets when the singlet is excluded; in flavor space we use the traceless octet matrix M^i_j. The weak c→q operator transforms as an SU(3)_F triplet H^i, with components H^1 = 0, H^2 ∝ V_cd, H^3 ∝ V_cs. The semileptonic hadronic Hamiltonian must be an SU(3)_F singlet constructed from the initial D (3bar), the light-meson octet M (8), the c→q triplet H (3), and the leptonic current. By SU(3) representation theory, there is a single flavor-invariant contraction (up to equivalent traceless projections), so at the flavor level there is one SU(3) irreducible amplitude for a given Lorentz structure. For pseudoscalar final states, the vector current contributes; for vector final states, vector and axial currents give distinct Lorentz structures, thus two independent reduced amplitudes in general. The task is to: (1) construct the SU(3)_F-invariant hadronic Hamiltonian from these building blocks and identify the irreducible amplitudes; (2) enumerate all allowed D-meson semileptonic decay channels into the light-meson octet (both P and V) and express each decay amplitude in terms of the irreducible SU(3) amplitudes and the appropriate CKM factors, without computing numerical values. For the light pseudoscalar (and analogously vector) octet, use the standard traceless 3×3 matrix basis M^i_j (rows label quark flavor i = u,d,s; columns label antiquark flavor j = u,d,s): M = [[(π^0/√2)+(η8/√6), π^+, K^+], [π^-, (−π^0/√2)+(η8/√6), K^0], [K^−, K̄^0, (−2η8/√6)]], and the analogous replacement for vector states ρ, K*, and the octet neutral ω8(≡φ8).
task_description: Construct the SU(3)_F-invariant hadronic Hamiltonian for D-meson semileptonic decays and use it to express amplitudes for all allowed decay channels. Specifically: (1) Define the SU(3)_F tensors for the D-meson anti-triplet D_i, the light-meson octet M^i_j (traceless), and the weak transition triplet H^i with H^1=0, H^2∝V_cd, H^3∝V_cs. Write the most general SU(3)_F-invariant hadronic Hamiltonian density for semileptonic decays, separating possible Lorentz structures (vector and axial) and identifying the corresponding SU(3) irreducible amplitudes. (2) Enumerate all semileptonic decay modes D^0, D^+, D_s^+ → (light pseudoscalar octet) ℓ^+ ν_ℓ and similarly for the light vector octet, omitting SU(3) singlets. Using the Hamiltonian, express each channel’s amplitude in terms of the irreducible amplitude(s) times the appropriate CKM factor and the SU(3) Clebsch coefficients given by the octet matrix entries (including the √2 and √6 factors for neutral states). Do not compute numerical values; present analytic amplitude relations only.

expected_output.description: 1) The SU(3)_F-invariant hadronic Hamiltonian for D semileptonic decays, displaying the unique flavor-invariant contraction D_i H^j M^i_j (with the traceless projection implicit) multiplied by the leptonic current, and identifying separate reduced amplitudes for P and V final states (e.g., A_P and A_V, A_A). 2) A complete list of allowed channels: for P = {π^±, π^0, K^±, K^0, K̄^0, η8} and V = {ρ^±, ρ^0, K^{*±}, K^{*0}, K̄^{*0}, ω8(=φ8)}, organized by initial state D^0, D^+, D_s^+. 3) For each channel, an amplitude expression proportional to the corresponding CKM element (V_cd or V_cs) times the appropriate reduced amplitude and SU(3) coefficient (including factors like −1/√2, 1/√6, −2/√6 for neutral octet members).

conservation_law: Lepton number conservation in each lepton family
Color confinement (final observed states are color singlets)
constraints.spatial_scale: Hadronic scale ~1 fm; processes localized at weak vertex
constraints.time_scale: Charm hadron lifetimes ~10^−12 s; semileptonic decay timescale
constraints.energy_scale: GeV scale (m_c ~ 1.3 GeV); CKM-suppressed transitions via V_cd, V_cs
constraints.initial_condition: Initial state is a charmed meson D_i at rest or with definite momentum; final state contains one light meson (octet) plus ℓ^+ ν_ℓ
mathematical_constraints: SU(3) tensor analysis and Wigner–Eckart theorem
Weak V−A current structure (separate vector/axial reduced amplitudes if needed)

input_data.description: No external numeric input is required; purely analytic SU(3) flavor analysis.

sub_tasks.description: Define SU(3)_F field content and conventions: D_i (i=1,2,3) as D^0, D^+, D_s^+; light-meson octet matrices for P and V (with explicit entries for π, K, η8 and ρ, K*, ω8/φ8); weak triplet H^i with H^1=0, H^2∝V_cd, H^3∝V_cs; leptonic current factor.
sub_tasks.expected_output: Clear mapping between tensor indices and physical states; explicit octet matrices with coefficients (√2, √6) for diagonal elements; statement of CKM assignments for H^i.

sub_tasks.description: Construct the SU(3)_F-invariant hadronic Hamiltonian for semileptonic decays. Show that, at the flavor level, the unique invariant contraction is (D_i H^j − (1/3) δ_i^j D_k H^k) M^i_j, which reduces to D_i H^j M^i_j due to Tr M = 0. Separate Lorentz structures (vector for P final states; vector/axial for V final states) and define corresponding SU(3) irreducible amplitudes (e.g., A_P; A_V and A_A).
sub_tasks.expected_output: Analytic expression for the effective Hamiltonian density: Heff ∝ [A_P D_i H^j P^i_j] (ℓ̄ γ^μ ν) J_μ + [A_V, A_A terms for V final states], with SU(3) flavor indices and tracelessness noted.

sub_tasks.description: Enumerate all allowed D-meson semileptonic decay channels into the light octet and express amplitudes using the Hamiltonian. For pseudoscalars: D^0→{π^−, K^−}; D^+→{K̄^0, π^0, η8}; D_s^+→{K^0, η8}. For vector octet: D^0→{ρ^−, K^{*−}}; D^+→{K̄^{*0}, ρ^0, ω8(=φ8)}; D_s^+→{K^{*0}, φ8}. Attach CKM factors and octet coefficients (e.g., −1/√2 for π^0 and ρ^0; 1/√6 or −2/√6 for η8 and ω8/φ8).
sub_tasks.expected_output: Channel-by-channel amplitude relations, e.g., for P: A(D^0→π^−ℓ^+ν)=A_P V_cd; A(D^0→K^−ℓ^+ν)=A_P V_cs; A(D^+→π^0ℓ^+ν)=−A_P V_cd/√2; A(D^+→η8 ℓ^+ν)=A_P V_cd/√6; A(D^+→K̄^0ℓ^+ν)=A_P V_cs; A(D_s^+→K^0ℓ^+ν)=A_P V_cd; A(D_s^+→η8 ℓ^+ν)=−2 A_P V_cs/√6. Analogous expressions for vector final states with A_V and/or A_A.
